events.title: Pitch Please #9
events.description:
***Registration opens mid-June***

Join us for an energizing afternoon of pitches, networking, and deep dives into some of Seattle's most promising early-stage AI startups!

📅 When: Once a month, 2.5-hour afternoon sessions

✨ What’s Happening?

Pitch Session (1.5 hours)
Five talented founders take the stage selected from the AI2 Incubator applicant pool (apply [here])
Each founder gets 15 minutes total:
• 3 minutes to pitch
• 6–7 minutes of candid feedback and Q&A from AI2 Incubator experts, including:
Yifan Zhang Managing Director, AI2 Incubator

Dan Koch, Founder, Lux Radium; AI House Venture Partner

Frank Chang, Co-Founder and Managing Partner, Flying Fish Partners

Plus, 3 minutes of live audience Q&A for each founder—questions selected by our moderator.
Networking Mixer (1 hour): Stick around afterward to network with founders, potential co-founders, investors, and AI enthusiasts.

🚀 Audience Participation:

Audience votes on their favorite pitch—"Audience Choice" winner automatically receives a guaranteed 1:1 meeting with AI2 Incubator.

🔑 Post-Event Opportunities:

AI2 selects which of the pitching founders (could be all 5) to invite for follow-up 1:1 meetings, exploring potential investment and incubation.

Whether you're pitching, exploring co-founder opportunities, or simply looking for inspiration, join us and elevate your startup game!

Please note: These events are not open to investors or folks from banking.
We do invite one guest VC to join the panel each time — if you’d like to be considered, feel free to email us directly!

Please note: RSVPing or receiving an invite approval via Luma does not mean you’ve been selected to pitch. If you’re chosen, someone from the AI2 Incubator team will reach out to you directly with next steps.

events.title: Moms Who Build: Maternal Health Hackathon — An AInChai + Armoire Meetup
events.description:
Moms Who Build — An AInChai Meetup

☕ Coffee + AI + Community

We're moms. We meet. We talk about our real lives — the messy, beautiful, exhausting, hilarious parts. And then we build something.

This July we're doing something a little different.

Maternal health and mom-focused organizations are bringing real, unsolved problems into the room — the gaps they see every day, the things that don't exist yet, the solutions they don't have the technical capacity to build.

And we're going to build them. Together. That night.

Nobody understands what needs to be built for mom life better than the people living it. So this time, moms and builders are the ones in the driver's seat.

Here's how the night works:

Organizations present real problems — 3 minutes each, no fluff. Then we form teams — moms, builders, anyone who wants to help — and spend 60 minutes building a solution using AI tools. A landing page, a workflow, a prototype, an app. Something real, not a slide deck.

Every team presents back to the organization at the end.

You don't need to be technical. You don't need experience. You just need to show up.

The night includes:

A fireside conversation about building something while raising someone — the honest version, not the highlight reel.

A build sprint — real problems from real organizations, real teams, real output in 60 minutes.

On-site babysitting provided by Rosie — because you shouldn't have to choose between showing up and being a mom. Kids are welcome and cared for so you can fully be in the room.

Light bites. Good company. The kind of room where you feel like yourself again.

Who this is for

Moms who are building something — or want to. Builders who want to work on something that actually matters. Anyone who believes the people closest to a problem are the best ones to solve it.

What to bring

A laptop. Curiosity. Free Lovable credits will be provided for the build session. And if you have little ones — bring them too. Rosie has babysitting covered on site.

Come as you are. Leave having built something real.

🔁 Prompt. Plan. Build. Repeat.

🌐 www.ainchai.com

Sponsored by:

Lovable — The AI-powered building platform. The build credits provided for every attendee

Armoire — designer clothing rental, proudly hosting Moms Who Build at their SODO boutique

Childcare partner: Rosie — On-site babysitting so every mom can be fully present.

​​About AInChai

AInChai helps non-technical builders learn AI, ship products, and grow — through training, consulting, and tools made for real people. Learn more at www.ainchai.com

By registering for or attending this event, you consent for the hosts and its affiliates to use your name, image, and likeness in any marketing related to the event. This consent includes, without limitation, an irrevocable, perpetual, worldwide, fully-paid, royalty-free, sub-licensable, non-transferable license to use any photographs or video recording on you taken while in attendance at the event. You also consent to receiving emails from the event hosts and sponsors.

events.title: Speed Dating for Ppl Who Always Need a Trip Booked [28-35 yo] | Men & Women | Capitol Hill
events.description:
​​🗣️🗣️ Calling you over if this event falls between trips on your calendar 🗣️🗣️

IT'S SUMMER, aka time to get off the apps and into the city—join us for an evening of low-key speed dating! Bonus: your night out for the plot also supports Sugar Hill, a local BIPOC-owned business at the same time. Win win.

​​❓Who is this event for? ❓

All of our events are made for BIPOC-identifying folks in the Greater Seattle area! This evening in particular is for men and women (ages 28-35) that constantly need a trip booked to feel ✨truly alive✨!

Come compare destination and itinerary notes and maybe find your future travel partner.

❓ How does it work? ❓

Mix Mix speed dating events run without an in-person host announcing table switches on a mic and definitely no nametags. Instead, your evening is guided virtually (via text!) and we set you up on meet-cute style dates (i.e., "Your next date is named Arnold, look for a football-shaped head with a tiny hat, and a blue sweater over red flannel").

At the end of the event, you review all your dates and tell us who you felt a potential connection with. If they also pick you, we give them your first name and phone number and then y'all take it from there!

​​​​🎟️ How much does it cost to Mix Mix? 🎟️

This will run you an $20 ticket plus at least one drink at the bar. We don't include drinks with our ticket price because A) We don't want to restrict your bevvy choices, and B) $$$ accessibility. Drinks range from $8 to $16, so we're leaving how much your night costs up to you. Maybe you want one! Maybe you want three! ¯\_(ツ)_/¯ Just keep your consumption respectful!

​​🌙 The night in a nutshell 🌙

You arrive, check in, and buy a drink to support our gracious hosts for the night.

You have several 10-min dates with brief breaks in between, and we supply starter questions/topics so your evening is filled with thoughtful conversation.

At the end of the event, we send you a form to review your dates and tell us who you liked!

We send your matches within 24 hours.

​​What we expect from you:

Be open to all kinds of connections—not everyone is the love of your life but everyone IS worthy of a chat.

You buy a drink to support our gracious hosts for the evening, and thereby support a local BIPOC-owned business.

​​What you can expect from us:

Privacy! Only your first name and phone number are shared with matches. Consent is everything—we're not sharing your info with anyone unless the vibes are mutual.

A chill time and some suggested convo topics for each date.

​​Fine print:

Cancellation policy: In order to respect everyone's time, money, and experience, there are no refunds and cancellations permitted. Additionally, if you are more than 5 minutes late, you will be counted as a no-show. All no-shows incur a $50 fee. See our full terms and conditions.

SMS disclosure: By registering for this event, you agree to receive transactional text messages from Mix Mix about this event, including registration confirmations, event reminders, check-in instructions, and match notifications. Message frequency: 8-12 messages per event. Message and data rates may apply. Reply HELP for help or STOP to opt out.

​Learn more about Mix Mix:

Mix Mix 101

Event day FAQ
hosts: Mix Mix | Speed Dating in Seattle

events.title: The Carry x Wavekelly: Walk, Shop & Matcha
events.description:
Get ready for round two.

We're back on Bainbridge Island with our friends at Wavekelly—

✨ 2.5 mile island walk + strength circuit by local fitness trainer
🛍️ Shop Wavekelly's latest summer styles 
​💪 Try on + purchase The Carry weighted vests (2 colors—sizes 1, 2, 3)
🍵 Matcha by Vale + good vibes
🎁 Special offers & free gifts with purchase

Last year 50+ of you showed up. How many can we round up this year? Bring a friend. Bring your vest and try on ours. Mingle with the founders, the community, and other women who like to move in style.

🗓 Saturday, July 11th
⏰ 10:30 AM (depart for walk at 11:00AM)
📍 Wavekelly > island waterfront > park strength circuit > back to Wavekelly

RSVP now—let's move!

—

🚢 Getting to Bainbridge

If you're coming from Seattle, take the 9:35 AM Seattle ferry.
You’ll land around 10:05 and be at the store by 10:15—perfect for our 10:30 AM start. If the ferry runs late, no stress—we’ll wait for you. Island locals—see you right on time 😉.

​🚶‍♀️ The Route

We’ll be walking a beautiful 2.5-mile route curated by Megan of Wavekelly. Route Link📍 Stroller-friendly? Absolutely. You’ll just veer off slightly before we hit the Sakai Trail (at 305 & High School Rd) and rejoin us at the new Sakai Park Tennis Courts for a group photo!

​💪 Post-Walk Burn

After the walk, stick around for a 20-minute guided strength session in the park led by Kristin DaFoe from Lighthouse Fitness. Think of it as the cherry on top—just the right kind of burn to leave you feeling strong and energized.

📸 Photo + Video

We’ll have a photographer capturing our strong, stylish community. Snap your own too and don’t forget to tag us: @thecarry.co & @shopwavekelly

​🎽 Don’t Have a Vest?

No worries! We’re bringing a handful of samples—first come, first serve—pass them along to another vestie, and just move with us.

events.title: Seattle Code & Coffee @ Internet.Dev!
events.description:
Code and Coffee is an inclusive, informal co-working session. People of all skill levels attend, and we love it that way. Many people bring projects to work on, and many other people socialize the entire time. It's entirely up to you!

👋 The Introduction Circle
Around 30 minutes into the event (12:30 pm), we will do a standup in a circle.
Round 1:

Your name

Your occupation/professional interests

What would you like help with?

Round 2:

Job opportunities you're hiring for OR announce that you are looking for one. If not, that's cool.

Community events you wanna plug

After the intro circle, everything is self-organized!

🛠️ Tech Workshops
We offer the following workshops after the intro circle. These are free for all and hosted by friends/volunteers at C&C! If you have specialty knowledge you'd like to share, or wish to find an audience for your tech talk, please reach out to any of our volunteers or @ us on discord!

Securing AI Coding Agents: Sandboxes, Trade-offs, and Developer Experience - Yason Khaburzaniya

AI coding agents let you code 10 times faster than ever before, but running them safely is not a trivial task. We will go over dangers of running unsecured agents and the trade offs when securing them. Attendees will learn the steps required to properly secure their coding agents inside sandboxes without sacrificing developer experience.

☕ Free coffee!
Please RSVP to secure your spot and help us get an accurate count on coffee.

🅿️ Parking advisory
Unfortunately, no parking will be provided for this event. Plenty street parking options are availble near pike place market.

↪ Detailed location

Find us on...
Discord
LinkedIn
Website
We will be looking to take photos during the meetup. 📸 Let one of the organizers know if you would rather we not include you in the photos!

Find out more about Internet Development Studio's history, mission, and vision!

events.title: Escaping Flatland Meetup - Seattle
events.description:
Curious to meet other Escaping Flatland readers near you? 
Come meet, talk, and spend a relaxed afternoon together :)

We’ll meet July 11 at 1pm in Volunteer Park which is close to I-5 and accessible by public transit. There is an old tree between 15th Ave and the back of the Asian Art Museum which has a large, low-hanging limb. See you there!

Feel free to bring coffee, snacks, or a blanket to sit on. Children are welcome.

If it rains, we’ll decide what to do depending on how many we are and email you with an update.

events.title: Pilates In The Park
events.description:
Join us for Pilates in the Park, an outdoor reset surrounded by fresh air and open space. We’ll begin with a grounding 10-minute reflection, then flow into a 45-minute Pilates class taught by our wonderful instructor, Audrey.

Afterward, enjoy a Nonda matcha pop-up and a flower arranging station for a chance to get creative, and take a little piece of nature home with you.

Mats will be provided!!!!!

Refunds will not be provided! Be aware that if you join the waitlist, it will automatically charge you when you get accepted, so make sure to remove yourself from the waitlist if you can no longer attend.

We can’t wait to flow with you 🍃

events.title: PNW Climate Week Seattle: Opening Ceremony: Building Toward an Affordable, Resilient and Prosperous Green Economy
events.description:
Kick off a week of high-impact learning, collaboration, and climate action at our Seattle grand opening!

Discover home-grown solutions from regional leaders, and see how our Pacific Northwest community is outpacing state and federal benchmarks to build toward an affordable, resilient and prosperous green economy.

11:30 Arrival and Networking

12:00 Welcome - Jason Huff, President, PNW Climate Week; 
Opening Keynote - Lylianna Allala, City of Seattle's Office of Sustainability and Environment 

12:20  Look Ahead to the Week and Second Keynote - Chrissy Cooley, Executive Director, Puget Sound Clean Air Agency

12:35  Panel discussion: Best Practices from the Puget Sound Region: What’s Working, Challenges and Opportunities Ahead

1:15 Panel discussion: Resources for Building Climate Implementation Capacity Across Washington

1:55 Closing remarks

events.title: PNW Climate Week Kickoff Happy Hour, w. SVB & Climate Tech VCs
events.description:
Ready to kickoff PNW Climate Week? Join us on the Madison Centre rooftop for the official PNW Climate Week Kickoff Happy Hour! Hosted by SVB Seattle, SNØCAP, Stepchange, Velveteen, Actuate, Propeller, Conduit, Acequia, Gigascale, E8, Burnt Island, and WestRiverGroup(WRG); together, we’re celebrating the start of PNW Climate Week with summer vibes, good company, and unbeatable city views.

Swing by after work, soak up the sunshine, and connect with fellow climate techies over heavy appetizers, boozy slushies, SVB client beer and wine.

Let’s toast to making a difference!

By registering for this event, you agree to share your information with the event organizers, PNW Climate Week, and Climatebase / the Climate Week Network. You may receive updates from these organizations, and can opt out at any time in accordance with their respective privacy policies.

events.title: Sessions: Seattle
events.description:
1% for the Planet Sessions are designed to inspire environmental action and grow our global movement. We're showing up in local communities to engage with our members, give back to environmental partners, and inspire local businesses to join this incredible network. And we're bringing the energy to Seattle during PNW Climate Week. Are you with us?

Join us at Patagonia Seattle where you’ll:

Be inspired by powerful local partnership and impact stories

Meet and support local environmental partners (Adventure Travel Conservation Fund, Na'ah Illahee Fund, and NW Avalanche Center)

Hear about 1% for the Planet's vision for the future

Engage with 1% for the Planet member brands

This isn't your average networking event. It's a casual, meaningful space to gather with folks doing good work. Come solo or with friends - this event is open to the community!

Because we know that when our network comes together, incredible things happen.

What to Expect:

A relaxed, welcoming happy hour vibe

Inspiring conversations

A drink or two on us 🍻

Tickets:
$35 each. The cost of your ticket raises money for 1% for the Planet.

Members: Ticket purchase will automatically be added toward your 1% commitment.

Presented by:
1% for the Planet

events.title: Whatnot in Seattle
events.description:
Whatnot is the largest live shopping platform in the U.S. and Europe, with over $8B in live sales in 2025 and the #1 spot on Forbes' America's Best Startup Employers list. 

Our Seattle team is growing fast across every function, building live commerce at a scale that's never been done in the West. There's no playbook, and the people joining now are shaping how it all develops.

Join us at 2120, just steps from our Seattle office in the Denny Triangle, for an evening of drinks and networking with the people building Whatnot. You'll hear from Whatnot leadership and Washington-based sellers who've turned live selling into thriving businesses.

Come curious and see what live shopping is all about.

events.title: Don't just dream it, Build it.
events.description:
Join Seattle's product, design, engineering, and AI leaders for an evening of drinks, conversation, and custom Nike By You sneakers.

Your team has adopted AI somewhere in the stack - Cursor, Claude Code, Figma, you name it. ICs are moving faster, but delivery timelines haven't kept up. The time is disappearing somewhere in the coordination layer that none of those tools were built to fix.

On July 15th, Builder.io is hosting a group of senior product, design, engineering, and AI leaders in Bellevue for drinks, appetizers, and candid conversation about what's working and where time is going.

Each attendee will design their own pair of Nike sneakers, to be shipped or hand-delivered upon completion. A reminder that the best products, and the best experiences are never one-size-fits-all. They’re built with context, craft, and collaboration.

Space is limited. RSVP to reserve your spot.

events.title: Seattle AI Meetup - Post Alley
events.description:
Seattle devs, let’s get better together. 

This used to be a meetup about building web and mobile apps with React. And then we noticed that every talk was about AI. 

And people started building games, and tools, and terminals, and models...

So now it's an AI meetup. 

Whether you are deeply skill-pilled or just AI curious -this is the meetup to learn about practical use cases, fascinating experiments, and new tools you should try.  Most importantly it's where you'll meet the people working in and on AI every day. 

Who should attend?
Anyone trying to build software. This includes hardcore devs and the new generation of builders. 

Schedule

5:00 PM — Doors Open + meet & mingle

6:00 PM — Demos Start

After — Q&A + networking

🚪 Doors open at 5:00 PM
🎬 Demos start at 6:00 PM

events.title: Powering the PNW: Fission, Fusion, and the Future of Firm Clean Energy
events.description:
Washington State is sprinting toward a 100% clean grid by 2045, but the explosive energy demands of AI data centers and rapid electrification are straining our existing hydropower foundation.

Join us during PNW Climate Week to explore how next-generation nuclear technologies can deliver the clean, firm baseload power the region desperately needs.

🗓️ What to Expect

The AI Baseload Imperative: Why wind, solar, and batteries need a firm-power partner to keep the grid stable.

Scaling SMRs: The real-world timelines and economics of bringing Small Modular Reactors to Washington State.

Policy & Public Trust: Streamlining the siting process, aligning with state mandates, and prioritizing environmental justice.

🎤 The Panel

Zabrina Johal, CEO, Zap Energy (confirmed)
Leading the Everett-based startup's bold new dual-track strategy to commercialize both advanced fission microreactors and fusion technology.

Andrew Eckert, Director of Commercial Strategy, TerraPower (confirmed)
Developing advanced, molten salt reactor to bring carbon-free energy to the grid while generating isotopes for precision medicine.

Jackie Eustey, Strategic Outreach Program Manager - New Nuclear Development, Energy Northwest (confirmed)
Spearheading regional initiatives to deploy advanced SMRs and operating the Northwest’s only commercial nuclear plant.

Amí Hafkemeyer, Director of Siting and Compliance, Washington State Energy Facility Site Evaluation Council (confirmed) 
Providing the regulatory perspective on how state energy siting processes and timelines will affect advanced nuclear projects in the region.

🕒 Agenda

11:30 AM: Doors Open & Networking

11:40 AM: Introductions, Presentation & Panel Discussion

12:30 PM: Audience Q&A

12:45 PM: Networking

1:30 PM: Event Wraps

Location: SNBL Global Gateway, 2333 Seaway Blvd, Everett, WA 98203
Who Should Attend: Energy professionals, climate tech founders, policymakers, and grid enthusiasts.

By registering for this event, you agree to share your information with the event organizers, PNW Climate Week, and Climatebase / the Climate Week Network. You may receive updates from these organizations, and can opt out at any time in accordance with their respective privacy policies

events.title: State of Climate Tech VC in the PNW 2026 Round Table - Live Podcast Recording
events.description:
Where is climate capital actually flowing in 2026 — and where has it stalled?

Five investors working across the climate tech landscape sit down for an unscripted roundtable on the state of the market: what's getting funded, who's financing first-of-a-kind deployment, whether AI and climate are genuinely converging, and how the Pacific Northwest stacks up as an ecosystem.

This is a conversation, not a panel of prepared remarks. Each investor brings a question they're wrestling with and puts it to the group.

Featuring:

Susan Su — Toba Capital; co-host, Climate Money podcast

Ben Eidelson — Stepchange Ventures; host, The Stepchange Show

Jonathan Azoff — SNØCAP Venture Capital

Gabriel Scheer — Elemental Impact

Christine Boyle — Burnt Island Ventures

Facilitated by Gregory Heller, Foster School of Business and host of the Conversations on Careers and Professional Life podcast.

Getting to Founders Hall

Address: 4215 E. Stevens Way NE, Seattle, WA 98195-3200

Light Rail

U District Station — 8-minute walk (closest)

Husky Stadium Station — 20-minute walk

Bus

Many lines serve the campus. For stops and routes, see the UW transit map. Founders hall is a 5-minute walk from 15th Ave NE and NE 42nd St or NE 45th St, with bus stops serving many lines.

Parking

Street Scarce and time-limited near campus. Your best bet is 17th Ave NE, just north of campus.

On campus (expensive, limited)

N1 lot (Burke Museum) — closest pay lot. $5/hour, $21/day.

E3  E4 lots — $5day, but a long walk to Founders Hall. Good option if you're also visiting the Clean Energy Testbeds.

Biking

Covered and uncovered bike parking is available at Founders Hall.

events.title: Climate Change and Healthcare: Is the industry meant to heal us hurting us?
events.description:
UPDATE: TIME, DATE AND LOCATION CONFIRMED.

What does the healthcare industry have to do with climate change? More than you might think. Join us for a 45 minute presentation with time for Q&A that digs into one of the biggest paradoxes of the climate conversation: the very industry intended to keep us healthy might also be contributing to the crisis that threatens our health in the first place. We'll look at how climate-related diseases and illnesses are reshaping the medical world, and whether healthcare is rising to meet that challenge or quietly making things worse.

Speakers:

McKenna Parnes, PhD, MSEd is a clinical psychologist and researcher in Seattle, WA. Her work focuses on the intersections between youth mental health and climate change, with consideration of the ethical responsibility society holds in protecting and promoting the health and well-being of younger and future generations.

Adam Lough, MHA is a Sr. Strategic Advisor at the Wilshire group with a background spanning healthcare sustainability, revenue cycle, supply chain, and health systems management. His work is driven by the conviction that a healthier healthcare system, for patients, communities, and the planet, is achievable.

Agenda:

Doors open at 6pm.

Presentation begins at 6:15pm.

45 min presentation with Q&A.

Drinks and snacks will be available.

events.title: HackwithSeattle 2.0
events.description:
🎉 HackwithSeattle 2.0 – 8-Hour Hackathon at AWS Seattle 🎉

Join us for HackwithSeattle, an electrifying 8-hour hackathon bringing together the brightest minds in tech, creativity, and innovation — all under one roof at the iconic AWS Skills Center in Seattle!

🚀 Whether you're a seasoned hacker, a budding coder, a design wizard, or an idea machine, HackwithSeattle is your chance to build, learn, and connect with the most passionate problem-solvers in the city.

🗓️ Event Details

Date: 17th July 2026

Time: 08:30 AM – 4:30 PM

Location: AWS Skill Centre, Seattle

💡 What to Expect:

High-energy hacking sessions

Cutting-edge challenges

Swag, snacks & networking

Expert mentorship

Exciting prizes for top teams

A chance to demo your project to industry leaders

🔥 Limited seats available – register now and hack the future with us!

Let’s build something awesome together. 💻✨

events.title: Climate Karaoke 🎤 Presented by SNØCAP, Stifel Bank & Ashurst Perkins Coie
events.description:
🎭🎤 Climate Karaoke – Theater Edition 🎤🎭
To sing or not to sing? That is the existential question!

Need a break from that existential dread? The Original Climate Karaoke Party is back for PNW Climate Week! Join SNØCAP and Seattle's amazing Climate Tech founders, investors and community members for the most fun you'll have all summer (without a heat pump).

Oh and the venue this year? Yeah we outdid ourselves We managed to rent out AN ENTIRE THEATER! This feat of logistical magic would not have been possible if not for the generous contributions of our sponsors Stifel Bank and Ashurst Perkins Coie. Find Minh or Mike and show your gratitude by forcing them into an awkward duet. Or just get to know them and say thanks, you know, whatever works.

Important notes from the frontlines of fun:

🌋 Space is generous, but still limited. Approved guests are still subject to capacity caps at the door—so don't let your chance evaporate.

🌊 Warm up your pipes, but maybe skip the 20-minute hot shower.

Come dressed for a crowded room and great memories—vintage clothing and upcycled shwag encouraged.

See you on stage,
Shrina, Nate, and Jon
(the SNØCAP team – reminding you that working in climate is way more fun than your other job)

By registering for this event, you agree to share your registration information with the organizers of PNW Climate Week, receive communications about PNW Climate Week, and acknowledge that photos/videos may be taken for promotional use.

events.title: Pilates and Picnic in the Park: Crunch & Brunch x BODYROK Seattle
events.description:
Seattle summer is here!☀️ Join Crunch & Brunch and BODYROK Seattle for a wellness experience that blends movement, connection, and community. Start with an energizing mat Pilates class led by an expert instructor, then connect while enjoying a nourishing picnic spread. Come solo or with a friend, and leave feeling stronger, refreshed, and more connected. You'll also go home with some wellness goodies!

EVENT FLOW

Check In and Welcome

Gratitude Activity led by Miracle Olatunji, founder of Crunch & Brunch

Mat Pilates Class led by Lauren Banks, manager of BODYROK Seattle

Enjoy a picnic spread and make new friends!

Matcha by Vale Matcha (Pier 58)

Protein shakes by OWYN

*Bring a yoga mat and water bottle! We'll provide the rest.

MEET YOUR HOSTS

ABOUT US

Crunch & Brunch (@crunch.n.brunch) hosts events that combine fitness, food, and making new friends in Seattle. Our mission is all about connection: creating welcoming spaces to explore different forms of movement, discover wellness brands, and be part of a community that fosters joy, personal growth, and well-being.

BODYROK Seattle (@bodyrokseattle) is a modern fitness brand known for its high-intensity, low-impact workouts. Blending strength training, cardio, and core-focused movements, BODYROK classes are designed to sculpt, lengthen, and strengthen the body in just 40–50 minutes.

All tickets are final sale and non-refundable.

Release Agreements

Photo/Video Release: By registering and/or Waitinglisting for this event, you consent to having your photo or video taken, shared on public social platforms, and used for marketing purposes. If you would prefer not to have your picture or video taken, please let the organizers know onsite.

Hold Harmless Agreement: You understand and agree that you are attending the event and do not hold the organizers or affiliates liable for any damages or injuries you may incur during it.

events.title: Free Clothing Swap
events.description:
Join SSS for a special event during Summer at SAM! We'll be offering a free clothing swap alongside additional programming from the Seattle Art Museum and their awesome community partners!

This event is completely free and open to all, so share with your friends and help us spread the love!

events.title: GraphCon Seattle
events.description:
GraphCon is a focused, full-day gathering for the builders, architects, and data scientists pushing the boundaries of graph technology. We’re trading massive conference halls for a high-signal environment where the person sitting next to you is just as likely to have the answer to your scaling bottleneck as the person on stage.

We’re already locking in sessions on the frontiers of the graph stack:

GraphRAG & Graphs as Memory: Architectural patterns for the AI era.

Context Graphs & Hypervectors: Moving beyond simple nodes and edges.

Getting into Production: Real talk on crossing the chasm for enterprise implementations.

Closing AMA Reverse Hackaton (plus networking) where the attendees bring the problems, and our experts try to solve them live! Vaibhav Gupta will be our 'Architect-in-Chief,' moderating the chaos and keeping the collaboration on track!

Special thanks to our sponsors: 
Senzing, Neo4j, Google, Arango, Boundary, gdotv, and Generationship.

More Details: https://www.graphgeeks.org/graphcon

*This is a private event that requires pre-registration and approval. Ticket price of $45.00 includes 10.55% Washington State Sales Tax and 5% Seattle Admission Tax.

events.title: Dog Dash 5K | Thinkspace
events.description:
Kick off Seattle Tech Week with your favorite furry co-founder! Before we dive into a week full of keynotes, pitch competitions, and product launches, we are taking over the pavement for a high-energy 5K run and walk.

Whether you're a seasoned marathoner, a casual walker, or just a dog enthusiast looking to network, the Dog Dash 5K  is the perfect way to get outside, connect with the Seattle tech community, and celebrate the doggos of the industry.

events.title: Scaling Startups and GTM Strategies with Subhadeep Chatterjee, Global Investor Partnerships @ Google Cloud | Thinkspace
events.description:
Drawing on Subhadeep’s background as a 2x founder, former PwC consultant, and his current work supporting over 300 accelerators, he shares insights on:

Designing scalable sales and enablement motions for high-potential startups.

How founders and developers can leverage ecosystem partnerships and cloud infrastructure to accelerate growth.

Bridging the gap between complex engineering concepts and revenue-driving enterprise sales strategies.

Subhadeep Chatterjee: AI Builder (contact.new & vibeathon.live) & GTM Speaker | Global Investor Partnerships @ Google Cloud | 2x Founder | Startup Advisor and Investor | PwC GTM Consultant | Enterprise Sales VP/Executive | EE

events.title: Foundation Models Go Vertical | Ascend
events.description:
Every founder building on top of a foundation model is making the same bet: that the lab won't eat their lunch. This panel is where that bet gets stress-tested — by the people setting the rules at the labs, and the founder building directly in the blast radius.

Panel:

Moderator: Boaz Ashkenazy, founder & host @ Shift AI

Speaker: Ben Gaffney, deputy general counsel @ OpenAI

Speaker: Brian Hall, chief marketing officer @ Mistral AI

Speaker: Patrick Thompson, CEO @ Clarify

Capacity is limited, and priority will be given to venture-scale founders. RSVP early — this one will fill up fast.

events.title: Seattle Tech Week: Biotech Pregame | Bits in Bio
events.description:
Connect with Seattle's biotech community for sunshine and beers in downtown before starting your Seattle Tech Week adventures.

Bits in Bio connects the bioeconomy. We bring together innovators working at the intersection of software, AI, and biology. Our global community of over 12,000 members includes leading VCs, founders, and scientists.

events.title: Creativity, Intent, and the Future of AI // A TEDxSeattle Salon at Seattle Tech Week
events.description:
When machines can generate art, text, and music in seconds, what does it truly mean to be a creator?

Join us for a brilliant, multi-disciplinary panel discussion that moves past the usual AI hype to explore the deeper intersection of human intention and artificial intelligence. We are bringing together leading minds across music, contemporary art, neuroscience, digital authenticity, and ideas-led curation to unpack how we preserve our creative voices in a rapidly shifting landscape.

We’ll dive deep into the questions that matter most to the future of culture and innovation:

The Definition of Creation: How do we define creativity when machines can "create"?

The Root of Originality: In a world of infinite synthesis, what actually makes an idea original?

Guarding the Voice: How do creators leverage AI as a tool without losing their unique perspective?

Amplication vs. Intention: How can AI help ideas travel further while humans remain the core source of truth and intent?

Whether you are an artist, a builder, a researcher, or simply curious about the future of human ingenuity, this conversation will change how you view the tools of tomorrow.

The Panelists

Medearis Dixon – Grammy-Award Winning Musician

Barry Johnson – Renowned Contemporary Artist

Eric Scouten – Principal Scientist & Identity Architect, Adobe Content Authenticity Initiative

Dr. Chantel Prat – UW Neuroscientist & Author

huggingface & more (To be announced!)

Run of Show

6:00 PM – 6:15 PM: Doors Open & Welcome

6:15 PM – 6:35 PM: Panel Introductions & Creative Intersections

6:35 PM – 7:15 PM: Moderated Panel Discussion

7:15 PM – 7:35 PM: Audience Q&A (Submit your burning questions live during the chat!)

7:35 PM – 8:00 PM: Mix & Mingle

events.title: STW Official Kickoff Party Presented by Madrona, JPMorgan, & Vercel
events.description:
Join Madrona, J.P. Morgan, and Vercel to kick off Seattle Tech Week 2026 with an evening of great conversations, delicious bites, and pickleball at Picklewood in Sodo — Seattle's ultimate recreation destination to rally, relax, and refuel.

You don’t need to be a pickleball pro to join us. Come to play, learn, or simply enjoy the atmosphere and conversations. For those interested in giving it a try, though, pickleball pros will be on hand to help you learn the game.

This mixer is the perfect way to launch into a week dedicated to the innovation and talent that define the Seattle tech/founder ecosystem. Whether you're a founder, operator, investor, or simply passionate about tech, this is the place to kick off Seattle Tech Week.

Special thanks to SAP, Cooley LLP, Ellingson Group, and MongoDB for helping make this event possible.

events.title: The Network Effect: Women Building Community in Tech and Leadership | Armoire x Boss Babes
events.description:
The path through tech and leadership is rarely a solo climb — it's shaped by the people who open doors, share hard-won advice, and pull others up alongside them. But building that community can feel like its own full-time job, especially in fields where women are outnumbered and the most important opportunities travel through networks you may not yet have access to.

Join us for a panel conversation with the women building communities that solve exactly this. Our panelists are founders and leaders of organizations creating real connection, mentorship, and sponsorship for women in engineering, technical roles, and leadership — the kind of relationships that help you navigate big organizations, find your next role, and grow a career on your terms.

Expect honest stories, practical advice, and authentic connection. Parking is free. Light bites and coffee will be provided.

events.title: Women Founders & Funders Poker Night hosted by Project W & Armoire
events.description:
At most Seattle Tech Week events, you work the room. Here, you read it.

You'll sit across the felt from the founders, investors, and operators moving this ecosystem. Calling, raising, and bluffing your way into the kind of conversations a quick hello never gets you to.

No real money, but real stakes: who you walk out knowing.

Expert dealers run every table, so whether you've never held a hand or you play to win, you can participate.

Seats are limited - last year sold out!

About Project W
Project W helps women achieve positions of influence—as CEOs, business and professional leaders, and investors—where they drive positive change and make a lasting impact. We do that through our programs for women entrepreneurs, our peer communities, and our support of women funders.

About Armoire
Armoire is the Seattle-based, female-founded clothing rental membership that helps ambitious women love what they’re wearing. Armoire's curated recommendations and stylist picks save you time and make it easy to refresh your wardrobe in a sustainable way. From work to weekends, vacations to special events, you can rent styles for every occasion. Seattle members receive same-day deliveries or can visit our SODO member lounge to find the perfect styles to rent.

events.title: Stripe Developer Meetup Seattle July 2026
events.description:
Join us on July 28 for the very first Stripe Seattle developer meetup. This is an in-person event for anyone building with Stripe (or curious to start). An evening focused on Stripe user experiences, product insights, best practices, and community.

🍕🍺 Perks
Food and drinks provided!
🎫 Tickets
There is a hard limit for RSVP, with a waitlist operating on Luma
📍Location
The event is hosted at Stripe's Seattle offices,
920 5th Avenue, Floor 19, Seattle, WA 98104

📣 Talks

Agents with wallets: Why it's a thing - Lucas Dickey (Stripe)

Securing your Stripe Integration - Pranav Punjabi (Stripe)

Securely Handing Your Credit Card to an AI (No, Really) - Michael Liendo (Auth0)

🕚 Rough timings

6:00 pm - 6:30 pm: Doors open with refreshments

6:30 pm - 7:30 pm: Talks and Q&A

7:30 pm - 8:00 pm: Drinks, food, and networking

events.title: STW Startup Showcase at The Showbox (Live Demos) | J.P. Morgan
events.description:
Discover the Future of Innovation at the Seattle Tech Week Startup Showcase!

Seattle definitely has something goin' on. We count ourselves continuously the underdog when it comes to the talent to investment ratio... but it's time to change that. Word is out that Seattle has 23% of the country's AI engineering talent, and the startup ecosystem is growing fast!

Come check out what's going on up here. If you're an investor from out of state curious about Seattle, this is an event you won't want to miss!

Or perhaps you're a startup founder looking to connect with industry leaders and showcase your innovative ideas? Look no further! J.P. Morgan is thrilled to present the 2nd annual Seattle Tech Week Startup Showcase, a marquee event at the Showbox Theater dedicated to highlighting the region's most innovative founders and their companies.

Join us at the iconic Showbox Theater, where the hottest startups in the PNW will gather to demo, pitch, and network. We've partnered with local VC funds and Seattle ecosystem partners to curate a showcase featuring the best of the best in the PNW.

Event Highlights

On stage:

Early Stage Demos: This pitch competition will spotlight Angel to Pre-Seed startups

Product Demos: Experience cutting-edge innovations from Seed to Series C companies.

"Recently Raised": Cameos from the Founders of some of Seattle's Unicorn companies and biggest funding rounds in the last year  (new this year)

Investor Reverse Pitches from investors near and far!

At the Showbox:

Investor & VIP Section - we will have part of the house reserved for VIPs and investors so you will be sure not to miss the best demos in town!  (new this year)

Drink Tickets available for first 300 registrants

Schedule for the Startup Showcase (2:30–9:00pm)
Subject to change

2:30–4:30pm Startup Fair (Separate registration here)
4:30–5:30pm Break
6:00–9:00pm Live Demos 
8:30pm+ Live Music – Good Lettuce!

About your hosts

About J.P. Morgan

J.P. Morgan serves startups and venture-backed high-growth companies, founders and venture capital firms across the globe in industries like technology, fintech, disruptive commerce and internet, life sciences, climate tech and health tech. The firm provides deep industry expertise, local support, comprehensive solutions and services and a robust network of investors and partners to meet clients’ commercial, investment and private banking needs. Learn more at jpmorgan.com/innovationeconomy.

Brought to you in partnership!

This event is greatly supported by the efforts of many Seattle area VCs and organizations: Ascend VC, Madrona, AI House, Plug and Play Seattle, Anthos Capital, and more!

A big thank you to our additional financial sponsors: Google Cloud, Pilot, and Fenwick!

About Google Cloud: Go from idea to impact faster with Google Cloud, the AI hyperscaler for the agentic era. We are in a category of one, combining a complete, first-party stack with an open ecosystem to build your competitive edge. Apply for up to $350k in Google Cloud (and Gemini) credits.

About Good Lettuce: Seattle’s premier 80s and 90s rock cover band, featuring members from the VC, big tech and startup communities. Grab a drink and sing along. You’ll know 70% of the words to 90% of their songs.

events.title: Pitch & Run | Ascend
events.description: This will be a conversational-paced group run around Greenlake, where founders can join Ascend and other Seattle-area investors to informally share their startup idea. If walking is more your pace, we'll also have investors leading a walking group! Meet by the Greenlake basketball courts on the east side of the lake.

events.title: Life Sciences: The Science of Wine Tasting | SVB, Cooley, and Baker Tilly
events.description:
Join SVB, Cooley, and Baker Tilly for a relaxed happy hour during Seattle Tech Week, bringing together people across the Life Sciences and Healthcare space for great conversation, wine, and a chance to unwind with familiar faces and new connections. We’ll kick things off with a welcome pour and brief remarks, then hear a short, approachable talk from SVB client vintner Corliss/Tranche on the science of winemaking, followed by a guided tasting, light appetizers, and open networking.

The evening wraps with an exciting wine raffle and plenty of time to keep the conversations going. Whether you’re building in the ecosystem, advising companies, or supporting the space from another angle, come enjoy an evening that blends thoughtful conversation with a little celebration.

Agenda
4:00 – 4:30 PM – Guest arrival, check-in, welcome wine pour, and open networking
4:30 – 4:35 PM – Brief welcome remarks from SVB, Cooley, and Baker Tilly
4:35 – 4:50 PM – The Science of Winemaking, by SVB client vintner (Corliss/Tranche)
4:50 – 5:50 PM – Guided wine tasting, appetizers, and networking
5:50 – 6:00 PM – Raffle drawing and prize winner!
6:00 – 7:00 PM – Open Networking

Featured SVB Client Wine: Corliss / San Juan Vineyard / Secret Squirrel / Tranche

At Corliss, excellence is pursued with patience, precision, and an uncompromising attention to detail. From sustainably farmed estate vineyards to meticulous fruit selection, native fermentations, and extended aging, every decision is made with the singular goal of creating wines of depth, elegance, and longevity. Guided by founders Michael and Lauri Corliss alongside a dedicated team of winemaking professionals and the renowned Atelier Melka, our wines are released only when they are ready to be enjoyed at their fullest expression.

events.title: Huge Annual Summer BBQ | AI House
events.description:
OK folks, our biggest event of the year. You know it's always a good time!!!

We’re pulling together 700+ AI people — founders, engineers, investors, researchers, all of it — in one place for Seattle Tech Week.

There’s gonna be music, drinks, some bites, and just a lot of running into people you didn’t plan to meet but are very glad you did. We’ll also have a science fair-style setup in our new fancy ballroom with some of the coolest startups being built in Seattle right now 👀

It’s a party, but also kinda the whole point, bringing people together so you can actually learn, discover new things, and get more involved in what’s being built here.

Come hang!!!

Science Fair Teams TBA

Thanks to our sponsors: Google and JP Morgan!

About Google Cloud:

Go from idea to impact faster with Google Cloud, the AI hyperscaler for the agentic era. We are in a category of one, combining a complete, first-party stack with an open ecosystem to build your competitive edge. Apply for up to $350k in Google Cloud (and Gemini) credits.

About JP Morgan:

J.P. Morgan serves startups and venture-backed high-growth companies, founders and venture capital firms across the globe in industries like technology, fintech, disruptive commerce and internet, life sciences, climate tech and healthtech. The firm provides deep industry expertise, local support, comprehensive solutions and services and a robust network of investors and partners to meet clients’ commercial, investment and private banking needs. Learn more at jpmorgan.com/innovationeconomy.

events.title: Orbital Data Centers, Infrastructure and Connectivity with Ashurst Perkins Coie and Space Happy Hour
events.description:
Join Ashurst Perkins Coie and Space Happy Hour for a Seattle Tech Week event.

From launching the first AI chip in space to processing real-time data for civil, defense, and commercial customers, unpack how space-based infrastructure is becoming the next critical layer of AI and cloud computing. Grab a drink, ask your boldest questions, and connect with the community shaping the next frontier. Sign up before it fills up!

Schedule
👋📋5:00 p.m. – 5:30 p.m. PT: Check in 
🎤🙋5:30 p.m. – 6:15 p.m. PT: Panel discussion
🌅🍻6:15 p.m. – 8:00 p.m. PT: Sunset networking happy hour

Moderator
Ben Straughan, Ashurst Perkins Coie

Panelists

Derek Huerta, CEO, Eclipse Space
Philip Johnston, CEO, Starcloud
Brian Monnin, CCO & Co-Founder, Sophia Space

Ashurst Perkins Coie's Emerging Companies & Venture Capital lawyers are trusted advisors to the world’s most innovative companies and most visionary investors. We support startup clients from formation to exit with comprehensive legal and business counseling. Our clients have raised more than $15 billion in the private markets over the past three years between the pre-seed and growth stage. Our extensive corporate law experience, combined with sector-specific business and product counseling, ensure our services are tailored to each client's unique needs, investor expectations, and growth trajectories in cutting-edge industries like AI, aerospace and defense tech, fintech, digital health, gaming, energy innovation, digital media, and consumer packaged goods.

Space Happy Hours are informal gatherings of companies in the space industry as well as those in the broader space ecosystem. We offer lively events at breweries as well as exclusive events at space companies.

events.title: Seattle Tech Week: Casino Night for Founders, Builders, and Funders | MongoDB
events.description:
Seattle Tech Week, MongoDB, Madrona, NVIDIA, and Temporal are bringing together Seattle's top AI-native founders, builders, and funders for a night to test your luck!

Join us at The Ruins to close out Seattle Tech Week in style. ➡️

What's on the table:
🎰 ♠️♥️ Texas Hold'em Poker, Roulette, Blackjack & more!
🍸 Food and drinks
✨ A curated room of AI founders, builders, and startup funders
🤝 Meaningful networking across Seattle's startup and venture ecosystem
🏆 Exclusive prizes for the night's top chip earners!

How it works:
Every guest starts with an equal chip stack upon entering and can move freely between tables throughout the night. Play your hand at poker, blackjack, roulette, craps, and more as you build your stack and compete for top prizes awarded to the night's biggest chip earners.

Need more chips to stay in the action? You can reload by visiting one of the partner tables in the networking lounge/bar, then head back to the games and keep playing.

Tournament Prize Structure:
🥇 1st Place — NVIDIA RTX 4060
🥈 2nd Place — Apple AirPods Max
🥉 3rd Place — Meta Smart Glasses

Learn more about your event hosts:

MongoDB is a document database with the scalability and flexibility that you want with the querying and indexing that you need. Build, scale, and succeed with MongoDB for Startups.

Madrona is a venture capital firm that specializes in early-stage technology companies across the Pacific Northwest, Bay Area and beyond

NVIDIA is tackling challenges no one else can solve. NVIDIA's work ignited the era of modern AI and is fueling industrial digitalization across markets

Temporal is an open source platform that helps developers build, orchestrate, and scale resilient Enterprise and AI applications.

DISCLAIMER:

This is a casino-style entertainment event for networking purposes only. All chips are provided free of charge, have no cash value, and cannot be bought, sold, or redeemed for money. In accordance with Washington State law, no rake will be taken, no entry fees will be collected, and no cash prizes will be awarded.

events.title: System Override: Seattle Tech Week Rave - mpathic, Fuel Talent, SVB & Fenwick - 7:15 to 1 AM!!
events.description:
Seattle Tech Week’s most community building party is back — and this year we’re going full Y2K. This is the night where Seattle’s tech community trades laptops for lasers and takes over the dance floor.

Last year you danced the night away and left with meaningful connections that you couldn’t have made at a random tech happy hour. This year, we’re pushing the system to its limits: FULL SYSTEM OVERRIDE.

Step into a glitchy, high-energy rave with throwback Y2K vibes, immersive visuals, and nonstop music from DJs across tech and the local scene. Just like last year, all of our DJs and artists are local AI and tech builders, engineers, and, oh yeah, one VC.

✨ Custom light show by local artists building with visual AI tools 🍸 Free drink tickets for the first two hours 
 💫 Rave swag, surprises, and good energy

💿  DJ lineup: DJs Kirby, Daniel Sahn, Shep, Siyan, Quoth the Raver (Soundry AI), Cashmara (mpathic), Latinum and more! 

We heard you: Some improvements from last year include: lots of Air Conditioning and large fans! More drink tickets (with sponsors also handing out tickets throughout the night)! Faster door lines.

If you love EDM, techno, and dancing with the people building the future — this is your night.

Important details

21+ only

Each guest must RSVP individually

Capacity is limited

Come as you are! This means arrive straight from your other tech events OR change into comfy Y2K rave wear ahead of the rave.

We follow a PLURR code of conduct — Peace, Love, Unity, Respect, and Responsibility ❤️

Hosted by
SVB, Fenwick, Fuel Talent, mpathic and Soundry AI

Registration
Free entry + drink tickets with RSVP until 10pm; after 10pm doors also open to the public. 
21+ only — each guest must have their own ticket on Luma at entry.
